editorial_decisions.cover_headline: Fabs replace grids. Capital concentrates. Regulation fragments.
editorial_decisions.headline_rationale: Three dominant structural tensions across the six levers: the COMPUTE bottleneck migration from power to fabs, extreme CAPITAL concentration at the frontier, and divergent GOV approaches across US, EU, and China.

duplicate_cluster_decisions.decision: Kept Nadella/Suleyman restructuring signal. Other titles in cluster are unrelated academic papers incorrectly grouped. False positive due to broad entity matching.
Kept GPT-5.4 signal. Other OpenAI COMPUTE signals (Altman milestone, classified deal) are thematically distinct. No true duplicates among selected signals.
False positive. Cluster contains OpenAlex papers with 'meta' in titles (meta-analysis, metamorphic, etc.), not Meta the company. No action needed.
False positive. Academic papers about AI in various fields, not Intel-related. Entity matching too broad.
Two Google energy signals in top candidates. Kept 'Google's data center power playbook' (TechCrunch, more recent, broader scope). The Financial Times version covers same underlying deals.

editorial_decisions.energy_lever_note: Only 4 signal cards on ENERGY slide (10 total signals, 5 top candidates available, one was a misclassified Naval Ravikant quote about app-building that belongs in SOCIETY/INDUSTRY). Selected the 4 strongest energy-specific signals.
voice_checks: No em dashes used as connectors
No lists of three in headlines or summaries
No AI vocabulary (delve, tapestry, landscape as metaphor)
No hedging (might, could potentially, seems to suggest)
All ampersands escaped as & in HTML
Minus sign entity used for negative badges
